message:
perf(fsqlite-core): add SimpleCountIndexedRowidProbe fast path + top-category CTE join plan + prepared schema-identity refresh

Three related additions to the connection-level prepared-statement
fast-path machinery, all in fsqlite-core::connection:

## SimpleCountIndexedRowidProbe fast path

`prepared_count_indexed_rowid_probe_row` is the new evaluator for a
COUNT(*) shape we'd previously been falling back on the general
executor for: counting rows in a target table whose `column_index`
equals the rowid of every row of a probe table (optionally bounded by
`probe_rowid_upper_exclusive`). The probe rowids are collected from
either `iter_rows_in_rowid_range` or `iter_rows`, then for each rowid
we call `prepared_indexed_equality_rowid_count` and saturate-add into
a usize before converting to `i64` (with a `FrankenError::Internal`
guard if the count overflows i64).

The dispatcher branch that previously returned `Ok(None)` for
`PreparedQueryFastPath::SimpleCountIndexedRowidProbe { .. }` now wires
the new evaluator in and bumps the per-thread observability counter
already declared at module scope.

The `memdb_storage_count_shortcuts_safe` flag short-circuits this
back to the slow path when the connection's clean-file-backed memdb
invariants don't hold, so the fast path is opt-in by storage shape.

## Top-category CTE join plan

`TopCategoryCteJoinPlan` is a new compile-time plan struct
({product_root_page, category_column_index, name_column_index,
price_column_index, limit}) and a new
`FSQLITE_TOP_CATEGORY_CTE_FAST_PATH_HITS` per-thread counter. It
recognizes the SQL shape:

```sql
WITH top_cats AS (
  SELECT category, SUM(price) AS s FROM products
  GROUP BY category ORDER BY s DESC LIMIT N
)
SELECT name, price FROM products
WHERE category IN (SELECT category FROM top_cats)
```

The detector walks the query tree to confirm:
- exactly one CTE, no DISTINCT, no WHERE, no HAVING, no windows, no
  joins, two columns (group + SUM), one GROUP BY, one ORDER BY DESC.
- the CTE source table matches the outer table by name (label-aware),
  same schema scope, no index_hint, no time_travel.
- the outer SELECT references the same table with two column projects
  (name, price) labelled to the outer scope.

When all checks pass, the plan can short-circuit the materialization
of `top_cats` and run a single fused scan. When any check fails the
detector returns `None` and the general executor handles the query.

## Prepared schema-identity refresh

`ensure_schema_identity_unchanged_after_local_refresh` is a new
verification helper invoked after a local schema-cache refresh on a
prepared statement. It checks two independent signals:

1. `function_registry_generation` — if the connection's user-defined
   function registry has changed since the prepared statement was
   compiled, return `FrankenError::SchemaChanged` so the caller can
   recompile. Logs `function_registry_changed` to the statement-reuse
   event stream.
2. `schema_cookie` and `schema_generation` — only checked when the
   statement requires schema validation. Returns
   `FrankenError::SchemaChanged` and logs `schema_cookie_changed` or
   `schema_generation_changed` (whichever differs first).

Failure diagnostics include the prepared and current values of
whichever signal triggered the invalidation, formatted via the
existing `format_schema_identity` helper so the event log is parseable
by the reuse-event consumer.

## PreparedQueryFastPath classification helper

`PreparedQueryFastPath::can_use_clean_file_backed_memdb()` is a new
`const fn` that returns `true` for the fast paths whose evaluators are
safe under the clean-file-backed memdb invariants:
SimpleRowidLookup, SimpleProjectedRowidLookup,
SimpleIndexedEqualityLookup, SimpleRowidRangeScan,
SimpleCountStarRowidRange. Other variants (full-table scans, complex
joins, DISTINCT/GROUP BY) return `false` and remain on the general
path.

Tests: three new test groups (added at the existing test-mod offsets)
exercising the count-indexed-rowid-probe evaluator, the top-category
CTE detector / planner, and the schema-identity refresh failure
modes. No changes to the public API surface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
